## Tesla Drops Approximately 15%, Leading a Broad Technology Stock Selloff

A TSLA-led tech decline of this magnitude creates index-level pressure that can temporarily impair fundamentally sound names through forced de-grossing and ETF flows; a PM should separate sympathy moves in other holdings from genuine fundamental deterioration before reacting.

- Tesla shares fell approximately 15%, leading a wider decline in technology stocks.
- The move is framed as a cross-asset risk-off signal in the Globe and Mail coverage.
- TSLA's scale and index weighting amplified the broader market impact.
- The headline frames this as a sector-level event, not an isolated name move.
